hero.title: Hi! I'm Aziz
hero.description: I am a fourth-year Software Engineering student at Polytechnique Montréal with a strong interest in cloud computing, artificial intelligence, and applied mathematics. I previously worked as a web developer at PolyOrbite, a technical society, where I contributed to the development and maintenance of the organization’s website. More recently, I completed a software engineering internship at Poslik, a company providing point-of-sale (POS) solutions for restaurants. During this internship, I contributed to the development of an inventory management module by analyzing functional requirements, designing the technical architecture, and implementing core features such as quantity tracking, stock comparison, and automated adjustments. Working with PHP, Symfony, and PostgreSQL, I helped improve consistency between physical and digital inventories and streamline operational workflows. I am particularly motivated by projects that combine system design, cloud infrastructure, and data-driven solutions, and I enjoy building scalable, well-structured software systems.

skills.title: Skills
skills.math_desc: BFS, DFS, Dijkstra, A*; Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing; Calculus, Linear Algebra, Probability and Statistics, Optimization.
skills.other_desc: Data Analysis, CI/CD, Agile Development, Software Design, Operating Systems Kernels, Data Structures and Algorithms, Computer Networks, Docker and Virtualization, Databases (pgAdmin, Firebase, MongoDB), Linux development environment.

projects.title: My Projects
projects.rpg_stack: Electron, Angular, TypeScript, NestJS, MongoDB, AWS, Flutter, Dart, Firebase
projects.rpg_desc1: Developed a cross-platform multiplayer RPG platform with a desktop client (Electron) and a tablet mobile client connected to a shared backend.
projects.rpg_desc2: Implemented real-time client–server features (chat notifications, channel management, member permissions) using a NestJS backend and scalable microservices architecture.
projects.rpg_desc3: Led system design and architecture documentation (UML, system diagrams), contributed to CI/CD pipelines, and performed code reviews in an Agile team of 6.
projects.melanoma_desc1: Trained a custom CNN on 9,605 dermoscopic images to classify skin lesions as benign or malignant, achieving a ROC-AUC of 0.924 and F1-score of 0.865 on the held-out test set.
projects.melanoma_desc2: Mitigated class imbalance via computed class weights and threshold optimization (0.43 vs. default 0.50) to maximize recall, prioritizing sensitivity for malignant detection.
projects.ecoli_title: Predicting Antimicrobial Resistance in E. coli
projects.ecoli_desc1: Benchmarked 7 ML models on 1M-feature sparse genomic data for antibiotic resistance prediction in E. coli, achieving a public F1-score of 0.808 with the final ExtraTrees pipeline; results published in ICLR 2026 conference format.
projects.ecoli_desc2: Designed a 10-fold stratified CV pipeline with preprocessing embedded per fold (Chi² selection, SMOTE, variance thresholding) to prevent data leakage; tuned hyperparameters jointly using Optuna Bayesian optimization.
projects.bert_title: BERT for Medical Named Entity Recognition (ADE)
projects.bert_stack: Python, PyTorch, Hugging Face Transformers, NLP
projects.bert_desc1: Implemented BERT from scratch in PyTorch (109M parameters) for token-level BIO tagging, achieving 93.6% token accuracy and F1(ADE) ≈ 0.55 on a held-out medical corpus of 6,821 sentences.
projects.bert_desc2: Benchmarked custom BERT against fine-tuned BERT-base (F1 ≈ 0.87), analyzing the performance gap through the lens of transfer learning and class imbalance (~86% outside-entity tokens).
projects.gpt2_title: GPT-2 from Scratch for Machine Translation
projects.gpt2_stack: Python, PyTorch, Transformers, Large Language Models
projects.gpt2_desc1: Implemented a GPT-2 decoder-only Transformer from scratch in PyTorch (124M parameters) with full weight transfer from OpenAI pretrained checkpoints, achieving a 90/100 architecture compatibility score against HuggingFace reference.
projects.gpt2_desc2: Fine-tuned on 8,629 English–French sentence pairs using masked prompt loss and cosine LR scheduling, reaching BLEU-1 of 52.76 and SacreBLEU of 25.1 after 30 epochs.
projects.rag_desc1: Built a RAG pipeline for temporal QA over a Wikidata-grounded dataset (6,970 questions, 239,324 structured facts), combining LLaMA-3.2-3B-Instruct with dense retrieval via all-MiniLM-L6-v2; cosine similarity retrieval improved Exact Match F1 from 0.10 (zero-shot) to 0.28.
projects.rag_desc2: Fine-tuned the generator with Q-LoRA on instruction-formatted QA pairs and combined RAG + fine-tuning for the final architecture; evaluated zero-shot, few-shot, and RAG-augmented prompting modes.
